name: Arunachal Pradesh Labour Laws
overview: State-specific labour-law requirements applicable to establishments operating in Arunachal Pradesh. Independent State labour laws continue unless specifically repealed or replaced. Following commencement of the four Labour Codes on 21 November 2025, establishments must determine whether the Central Government or the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government for each obligation. Arunachal Pradesh has notified State rules under all four Labour Codes: the Industrial Relation (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021, the Code on Social Security (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021, the Code on Wages (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2022, and the Arunachal Pradesh Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Rules, 2022. Each set of rules commenced from the effective date of its corresponding Code.

authority.name: Department of Labour, Employment & Skill Development, Government of Arunachal Pradesh

rules.title: Review Arunachal Pradesh Shops and Establishments Registration
rules.description: Determine whether registration under the applicable Shops and Establishments legislation is required.

citation.reference: Applicable Arunachal Pradesh Shops and Establishments legislation

rules.title: Maintain Statutory Registers
rules.description: Maintain registers, records and notices prescribed under applicable labour laws.

rules.title: Display Statutory Notices
rules.description: Display notices required under applicable state labour legislation.

rules.title: Review Working Hours, Leave and Holidays
rules.description: Ensure compliance with state-specific provisions relating to working hours, weekly holidays and leave.

rules.title: Apply the Code on Wages and Arunachal Pradesh Rules
rules.description: Where the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government, apply the Code on Wages, 2019 together with the Code on Wages (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2022. Identify the applicable minimum wage, geographical area, employment and skill category, wage period and dearness allowance; observe working-day, weekly-rest, overtime, wage-payment, deduction and bonus requirements; and maintain the prescribed notices, wage slips, registers and returns.

citation.section: Chapters II to IV and sections 50, 67 and 69

rules.title: Apply the Industrial Relations Code and Arunachal Pradesh Rules
rules.description: Where the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government, apply the Industrial Relations Code, 2020 together with the Industrial Relation (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021. Assess trade-union, negotiating-union or negotiating-council, works-committee, grievance-redressal, standing-orders, service-condition, industrial-dispute, strike, lock-out, lay-off, retrenchment, closure and worker re-skilling requirements using the applicable statutory thresholds.

rules.title: Apply the Code on Social Security and Arunachal Pradesh Rules
rules.description: Where the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government, apply the Code on Social Security, 2020 together with the Code on Social Security (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021. Assess State-administered gratuity, maternity benefit, employees compensation, building and other construction worker welfare and cess, unorganised-worker registration, career-centre reporting and related social-security obligations. Apply provident-fund and employees-state-insurance requirements through EPFO and ESIC where those chapters and saved schemes apply.

citation.reference: Code on Social Security (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021

rules.title: Apply the OSHWC Code and Arunachal Pradesh Rules
rules.description: Where the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government, apply the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020 together with the Arunachal Pradesh Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Rules, 2022. Assess establishment registration, appointment letters, safety and health duties, medical examinations, welfare facilities, working hours and leave, employment of women, contract labour, inter-State migrant workers and sector-specific requirements using the applicable thresholds and saved instruments.

citation.reference: Arunachal Pradesh Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Rules, 2022
Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020

rules.title: Determine the Appropriate Government and Preserve Saved Actions
rules.description: For every establishment and compliance topic, document whether the Central Government or the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government. Apply the corresponding rules, notifications, wage rates, schemes, registrations, licences, forms, portals and enforcement authorities. Preserve prior registrations, licences, exemptions, proceedings, claims, liabilities, wage notifications and actions under repealed enactments only to the extent saved by the relevant Labour Code and not inconsistent with it.

citation.reference: Arunachal Pradesh Labour Code Rules and notifications
Implementation of the four Labour Codes from 21 November 2025
Appropriate-government, rule-making and repeal-and-savings provisions under the four Labour Codes

exceptions.title: Independent Arunachal Pradesh Laws Continue
exceptions.description: The four Labour Codes do not automatically repeal State legislation outside the central enactments expressly repealed by the Codes. Applicable State registration, holiday, leave, welfare and other independent requirements continue unless lawfully repealed or replaced.

exceptions.title: State Rules Apply Only Within State Jurisdiction
exceptions.description: The Arunachal Pradesh Labour Code Rules apply to establishments and matters for which the Government of Arunachal Pradesh is the appropriate government. Central Rules apply where the Central Government is the appropriate government.

exceptions.title: Rules Commenced with the Corresponding Codes
exceptions.description: The Arunachal Pradesh rules notified in 2021 and 2022 state that they come into force from the effective date of their corresponding Labour Code.

exceptions.title: Saved Legacy Instruments
exceptions.description: Rules, notifications, licences, registrations, exemptions, proceedings and actions under repealed enactments continue only to the extent preserved by the applicable Labour Code and until lawfully replaced, repealed or superseded.

recommendations.title: Annual Labour Compliance Review
recommendations.description: Conduct an annual review of compliance with applicable state labour laws.

recommendations.title: Digitise Labour Compliance Records
recommendations.description: Maintain electronic labour compliance records wherever legally permissible.

recommendations.title: Maintain an Appropriate Government Matrix
recommendations.description: Map each legal entity, establishment, industry and workforce category to the Central Government or Government of Arunachal Pradesh for each Labour Code obligation.

recommendations.title: Maintain a Labour Code Notification Register
recommendations.description: Track each State Labour Code rule, wage notification, amendment, form, authority appointment, portal requirement and effective date.

recommendations.title: Review Arunachal Pradesh Notifications Monthly
recommendations.description: Review official State and Ministry publications monthly for wage revisions, Labour Code amendments, schemes, exemptions, authority appointments and filing procedures.

sources.name: Department of Labour, Employment & Skill Development, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
sources.authority: Department of Labour, Employment & Skill Development, Government of Arunachal Pradesh

sources.name: Code on Social Security (Arunachal Pradesh) Rules, 2021
Arunachal Pradesh Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Rules, 2022

governance.changeSummary: Updated the Arunachal Pradesh record for implementation of the four Labour Codes without superseding independent State laws. Added the notified State rules under all four Codes, appropriate-government and repeal-and-savings controls, and retained the existing Shops and Establishments, documentation, notice, working-hours, leave and holiday requirements.
